description:
Configuration for API Access Control including authorization servers, resource servers, policies, and CORS settings. Properties to configure an API Authorization Server. An API authorization server typically defines one or more resource servers which have authentication requirements to permit access. This section can also be used to configure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) policies. 

**⚠️ Note:** When configuring APIAC resource servers, a bind credential must be provided. The automated configuration tool uses the `webseal.runtime.admin_user` and `webseal.runtime.admin_password` properties to authenticate. Therefore both of these properties **must** exist when configuring resource servers

authorization_servers.description: List of API Authorization servers to create.

resource_servers.description: List of API Resource servers to create.

policies.description: List of API access control policies to create.

cors.description: List of Cross-Origin Resource Sharing policies to create.

Authorization_Server.description: API Authorization Server configuration. Authorization servers are the points of contact for external traffic to access protected resource servers. Each server has its own object space in the Verify Identity Access policy server.

name.description: This is the new instance name, which is a unique name that identifies the instance.

hostname.description: The host name of the local host. This name is used when constructing the authorization server name.

auth_port.description: The port on which authorization requests will be received.

admin_port.description: The port on which Security Verify Identity Access administration requests will be received.

domain.description: The Security Verify Identity Access domain.

addresses.description: A json array containing a list of local addresses on which the authorization server will listen for requests.

ssl.description: Whether or not to enable SSL between the Security Verify Identity Access authorization server and the LDAP server.

ssl_port.description: The SSL port on which the LDAP server will be contacted. Only valid if ssl set to yes.

key_file.description: The name of the keyfile that will be used when communicating with the LDAP server over SSL.

key_label.description: The label of the certificate within the keyfile to use.

Resource_Server.description: API Resource Server configuration with junction and authentication settings. Resource servers are third party application servers / microservices that are being protected by the Authorization server.

reverse_proxy.description: Name of the WebSEAL Reverse Proxy instance this resource server is attached to.

server_hostname.description: The DNS host name or IP address of the target back-end server.

server_port.description: TCP port of the back-end third-party server. Default is 80 for TCP junctions and 443 for SSL junctions.

virtual_hostname.description: Virtual host name that is used for the junctioned Web server.

server_dn.description: Specifies the distinguished name of the junctioned Web server.

sever_cn.description: Specifies the common name, or subject alternative name, of the junctioned Web server.

description.description: An optional description for this junction.

junction_point.description: Name of the location in the Reverse Proxy namespace where the root of the back-end application server namespace is mounted.

stateful_junction.description: Specifies whether the junction supports stateful applications. By default, junctions are not stateful.

policy.description: The Policy that is associated with this Resource Server.

oauth_introspection_proxy.description: The proxy, if any, used to reach the introspection endpoint.

oauth_introspection_auth_method.description: The method for passing the authentication data to the introspection endpoint.

oauth_introspection_endpoint.description: This is the introspection endpoint which will be called to handle the token introspection.

oauth_introspection_client_id.description: The client identifier which is used for authentication with the external OAuth introspection endpoint.

oauth_introspection_client_secret.description: The client secret which is used for authentication with the external OAuth introspection endpoint.

oauth_introspection_client_id_hdr.description: The name of the HTTP header which contains the client identifier which is used to authenticate to the introspection endpoint. Only valid if client_id has not been set.

oauth_introspection_token_type_hint.description: A hint about the type of the token submitted for introspection.

oauth_introspection_mapped_id.description: A formatted string which is used to construct the Verify Identity Access principal name from elements of the introspection response. Claims can be added to the identity string, surrounded by {}.

oauth_introspection_external_user.description: A boolean which is used to indicate whether the mapped identity should correspond to a known Verify Identity Access identity or not.

oauth_introspection_response_attributes.description: A list of rules indicating which parts of the json response should be added to the credential.

static_response_headers.description: A list of header names and values that should be added to the HTTP response.

jwt_header_name.description: The name of the HTTP header that will contain the JWT.

jwt_certificate.description: The label of the personal certificate that will sign the JWT.

jwt_claims.description: The list of claims to add to the JWT.

junction_hard_limit.description: Defines the hard limit percentage for consumption of worker threads. Valid value is an integer from 0 to 100.

junction_soft_limit.description: Defines the soft limit percentage for consumption of worker threads. Valid value is an integer from 0 to 100.

basic_auth_mode.description: Defines how the Reverse Proxy server passes client identity information in HTTP basic authentication (BA) headers to the back-end server.

tfim_sso.description: Enables IBM Security Federated Identity Manager single sign-on (SSO) for the junction.

remote_http_header.description: Controls the insertion of Security Verify Identity Access specific client identity information in HTTP headers across the junction.

http2_junction.description: Specifies whether the junction supports the HTTP/2 protocol. By default, junctions do not support the HTTP/2 protocol.

http2_proxy.description: Specifies whether the junction proxy support the HTTP/2 protocol. By default, junction proxies do not support the HTTP/2 protocol.

sni_name.description: The server name indicator (SNI) to send to TLS junction servers. By default, no SNI is sent.

preserve_cookie.description: Specifies whether modifications of the names of non-domain cookies are to be made.

cookie_include_path.description: Specifies whether script generated server-relative URLs are included in cookies for junction identification.

transparent_path_junction.description: Specifies whether a transparent path junction is created.

mutual_auth.description: Specifies whether to enforce mutual authentication between a front-end Reverse Proxy server and a back-end Reverse Proxy server over SSL.

insert_ltpa_cookies.description: Controls whether LTPA cookies are passed to the junctioned Web server.

insert_session_cookies.description: Controls whether to send the session cookie to the junctioned Web server.

request_encoding.description: Specifies the encoding to use when the system generates HTTP headers for junctions.

enable_basic_auth.description: Specifies whether to use BA header information to authenticate to back-end server.

key_label.description: The key label for the client-side certificate that is used when the system authenticates to the junctioned Web server.

gso_resource_group.description: The name of the GSO resource or resource group.

junction_cookie_javascript_block.description: Controls the junction cookie JavaScript block.

client_ip_http.description: Specifies whether to insert the IP address of the incoming request into an HTTP header for transmission to the junctioned Web server.

version_two_cookies.description: Specifies whether LTPA version 2 cookies (LtpaToken2) are used.

ltpa_keyfile.description: Location of the key file that is used to encrypt the LTPA cookie data.

authz_rules.description: Specifies whether to allow denied requests and failure reason information from authorization rules to be sent in the Boolean Rule header (AM_AZN_FAILURE) across the junction.

fsso_config_file.description: The name of the configuration file that is used for forms based single sign-on.

username.description: The Reverse Proxy user name. Used to send BA header information to the back-end server.

password.description: The Reverse Proxy password. Used to send BA header information to the back-end server.

local_ip.description: Specifies the local IP address that the Reverse Proxy uses when the system communicates with the target back-end server.

query_contents.description: Provides the Reverse Proxy with the correct name of the query_contents program file and where to find the file. By default, the Windows file is called query_contents.exe and the UNIX file is called query_contents.sh.

case_sensitive_url.description: Specifies whether the Reverse Proxy server treats URLs as case sensitive.

windows_style_url.description: Specifies whether Windows style URLs are supported.

ltpa_keyfile_password.description: Password for the key file that is used to encrypt LTPA cookie data.

https_port.description: HTTPS port of the back-end third-party server. Applicable when the junction type is ssl.

http_port.description: HTTP port of the back-end third-party server. Applicable when the junction type is tcp.

proxy_hostname.description: The DNS host name or IP address of the proxy server. Applicable when the junction type is sslproxy.

proxy_port.description: The TCP port of the proxy server. Applicable when the junction type is tcpproxy.

sms_environment.description: Only applicable for virtual junctions. Specifies the replica set that sessions on the virtual junction are managed under.

vhost_label.description: Only applicable for virtual junctions. Causes a second virtual junction to share the protected object space with the initial virtual junction.

delegation_support.description: This option is valid only with junctions that were created with the type of ssl or sslproxy. Indicates single sign-on from a front-end Reverse Proxy server to a back-end Reverse Proxy server.

scripting_support.description: Supplies junction identification in a cookie to handle script-generated server-relative URLs.

force.description: Specifies whether to overwrite an existing junction of the same name.

resources.description: List of resources to add to resource server.

document_root.description: The document root defines a static set of web files (HTML, JS, CSS, ect.) which can be served by the Authorization server.

method.description: The HTTP action for this resource.

path.description: The URI path for this resource. This is a full server relative path including the junction point.

policy_name.description: The name of the custom policy if the type is custom.

static_response_headers.description: A list of header names and values that should be added to the HTTP response.

rate_limiting_policy.description: The name of the rate limiting policy that has been set for this resource.

url_aliases.description: A list of aliases that all map to the path of this resource.

doc_type.description: The value of the accept header that will trigger a documentation response.

doc_file.description: The name and path of the documentation file to respond with, relative to the junction root.

name.description: The name of the response header.

value.description: The value of the response header.

pos.description: The position of this attribute in the ordered list of all attributes.

action.description: The action to perform for this attribute.

name.description: The name of the custom policy if the type is custom.

type.description: The type of claim to add to the JWT.

value.description: The value for the claim. If the type is text this will be the literal text that is added to the JWT. If the type is attr this will be the name of the credential attribute to add to the JWT.

claim_name.description: The name of the claim that is added to the JWT. For attr type claims this is optional and if not specified the claim name will be set as the name of the credential attribute. If the type is attr and the value contains a wildcard this field is invalid and if specified will result in an error.

groups.description: The groups referenced by this policy. User must be a member of at least one group for this policy to be authorized. The default is no groups if not specified.

attributes.description: The attribute matches referenced by this policy. Each attribute must be matched for this policy to be authorized. The default is no attributes if not specified.

Cross_Origin_Resource_Sharing.description: CORS policy configuration. CORS policy can be used to configure the URI’s which are permitted to make cross-origin resource requests as well as the types of resources which are permitted to be shared.

name.description: The name of the CORS policy.

allowed_origin.description: An array of origins which are allowed to make cross origin requests to this resource. Each origin must contain the schema and any non-default port information. A value of * indicates that any origin will be allowed.

allow_credentials.description: Controls whether or not the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header will be set. If not present, this value will default to false.

exposed_headers.description: Controls the values populated in the Access-Control-Expose-Headers header.

handle_preflight.description: Controls whether or not the Reverse Proxy will handle pre-flight requests. If not present, this value will default to false.

allowed_methods.description: Controls the methods permitted in pre-flight requests and the subsequent Access-Control-Allow-Methods header. This option only relates to pre-flight requests handled by the Reverse Proxy and will be ignored if handle_preflight is set to false. Methods are case sensitive and simple methods (ie. GET, HEAD and POST) are always implicitly allowed.

allowed_headers.description: Controls the headers permitted in pre-flight requests and the subsequent Access-Control-Allow-Headers header. This option only relates to pre-flight requests handled by the Reverse Proxy and will be ignored if handle_preflight is set to false.

max_age.description: Controls the Access-Control-Max-Age header added to pre-flight requests. If set to zero, the header will not be added to pre-flight responses. If set to -1, clients will be told not to cache at all. If not present, this value will default to 0.
